title: CLAUDE.md — datasetviewer
text: datasetviewer — interactive, SAS Studio-style dataset viewer R. fast canvas grid column-selection panel, per-column property metadata, names-versus-labels header toggle, header-driven sort, free-text row filter (SAS “Filter Table Rows” style) full dataset row sampling. One htmlwidget codebase renders Shiny (primary target) static Quarto/HTML. Integrates sibling artoo package CDISC column metadata; plain data frames work zero artoo dependency. Note: always-dplyr-code box dropped (2026-06-18) favour SAS-style free-text filtering, code view re-added day -demand “Show code” toolbar button (modelled SAS Studio’s “Display code creates current table”). emits runnable, air-formatted dplyr pipeline current view (select / filter / arrange), generated JS (srcjs/codegen.js); free-text filter code view coexist. Global directives load ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md. file holds project-specific shared conventions. Deeper working detail, competitor framing, staged plan live CLAUDE.local.md (gitignored).

title: CLAUDE.md — datasetviewer
text: Transport: Parquet via nanoparquet, base64 widget payload. Engine: DuckDB-WASM, single engine, reads parquet natively, SQL filter/sort/select, unbounded scale. Grid: Glide Data Grid (canvas), virtualized; getCell pulls visible window via LIMIT/OFFSET.
code: R                                   browser (one htmlwidget) data.frame | artoo frame | path     state.js (single store)   -> .dv_columns_meta() (artoo)       -> sql.js (filter expr -> WHERE, sort -> ORDER BY)   -> nanoparquet -> base64 payload    -> engine_duckdb.js (DuckDB-WASM, native parquet)                                        -> grid_view.js (Glide Data Grid, canvas)                                        -> shell/ (toolbar, columns panel, property panel,                                                   filter_dialog, context_menu, export)

title: CLAUDE.md — datasetviewer
text: Strictly don’t lazy. Root cause patch, best long-term solution lazy shortcut. silent truncation, “good enough now.”

title: CLAUDE.md — datasetviewer
text: Anti-grinding. Verify stage adversarially advancing. check fails way twice, stop re-derive first principles instead retrying fix. backward-compat (pre-v1). Freely delete/rewrite earlier-stage code tests later stage shows simpler shape. Simplest design works; promote abstraction second concrete need forces .

title: CLAUDE.md — datasetviewer
text: OOP: S7 domain class ever needed (none yet). UI widget package stateful domain objects, S7 classes; viewer returns htmlwidget (S3) rest plain functions. real class appears, use S7 (R/aaa_class.R first, S7::method() dispatch, S7::methods_register() .onLoad) per house standard. add S7 scaffolding speculatively. Lightweight deps. Base R + targeted: cli, rlang, S7, htmlwidgets, htmltools, nanoparquet. tidyverse Imports. arrow banned (heavy C++); nanoparquet parquet engine. Errors via cli. cli::cli_abort(class = "datasetviewer_error_<kind>", call = rlang::caller_env()); kinds input, transport, engine, metadata. 3-part message; ASCII-inside cli strings. Naming. snake_case; bare exports (dataset_viewer, datasetviewerOutput); dot-prefix internals (.dv_normalize), ordinary locals. Don’t shadow base R. Base pipe |>; vapply; seq_along/seq_len; ::-qualify; library() R/. Test-first. testthat ed.3; bug fixes ship failing-first regression test issue ref. Error tests use expect_snapshot(error = TRUE) expect_error(class = ...). Pin codegen + payload schema snapshots. Fixtures via tibble::tribble(), never expand.grid() + $<-. Internals via datasetviewer:::. Coverage >= 95% per R/ file. Roxygen ~/.claude/rules/roxygen.md (exceed tidyverse/r-lib/gt): one @examples block, two progressive narrated examples, last expr renders; \dontrun/\donttest; CDISC clinical-canon examples. @section Syntax/Examples/Errors, per-param sub-tables, @family. Browser-launch CRAN trap (critical viewer). auto-printing htmlwidget routes htmltools:::html_print(), ignores interactive() launches browser -> calibre-XXXX detritus -> CRAN reject. Never end example/vignette/test unguarded auto-printing widget; ship test-examples-phantom.R. Examples run R CMD check, < 5s, tempfile() /O, never leave temp files.

title: CLAUDE.md — datasetviewer
text: srcjs/ -> esbuild -> committed inst/htmlwidgets/datasetviewer.js (package installs without Node). Rebuild: Rscript tools/build.R (needs one-time npm install). DuckDB-WASM + worker ship inst/htmlwidgets/duckdb/.

title: Get started with datasetviewer
text: Hand dataset_viewer() data frame. entire API common case — everything else interaction inside widget. Try : drag scrollbar, drag column border resize, click row. grid live — real widget, screenshot.

what: A guided tour of the interface
title: Get started with datasetviewer
text: layout mirrors SAS Studio, anyone used viewer already home: Columns panel (left) — checklist every column type chip (character, # numeric, calendar dates). Uncheck column hide grid; data never reloaded. Property pane (lower left) — select column inspect Label, Name, Length, Type, Format, Informat, attributes PROC CONTENTS reports. Toolbar (top) — names-versus-labels View dropdown, Export current view CSV button, Show code button (<>) reveals dplyr pipeline current view, Filter table rows (funnel) badge showing active filter. Header menu — right-click column header sort ascending descending, add filter, copy column, size columns content. Status bar — total row column counts, filtered count filter active. Tip Sorting filtering driven widget, R arguments, reader report can explore data without re-running code.

what: CDISC metadata, labels, and the property pane
title: Get started with datasetviewer
text: plain data frame labels, property pane shows names . Point viewer labelled CDISC-conformed frame metadata comes life. companion artoo package installed, column labels, formats, informats, storage lengths read straight frame shown property pane — can set header row use labels instead names. Select STUDYID columns panel property pane reads Study Identifier; header row now shows labels view = "labels". artoo dependency required plain frames — consulted present. Note dataset_viewer() also accepts path dataset file (dataset_viewer("adsl.parquet")); artoo::read_dataset() reads , carrying metadata property pane.
code: # artoo ships the CDISC pilot ADaM datasets used across these docs. dataset_viewer(artoo::cdisc_adsl, view = "labels")

title: Get started with datasetviewer
text: two ways filter, operating every row: Filter Table Rows — click funnel toolbar type free-text expression, SAS-style, AGE >= 75 SEX = "F". translated SQL clause, status bar updates matched count. Add Filter — right-click column header. dialog adapts column’s type: checklist distinct values character columns, comparison operator value numbers, date picker dates. filter runs DuckDB full Parquet payload, answer exact — matched count true count, count within sampled window.

title: Get started with datasetviewer
text: Exploration grid convenient, report needs reproducible. Show code button (<> toolbar) opens dialog runnable dplyr pipeline reproduces current view — column selection, filter, sort, order: snippet air-formatted syntax-highlighted, Copy button. SQL idioms translated R equivalents — (...) becomes %% c(...), becomes !x %% c(...), date time literals become .Date() / .POSIXct() / hms::as_hms() — code runs -source frame. modelled SAS Studio’s “show code creates table”, stays sync view: change filter sort reopen see updated pipeline.
code: mtcars |>   dplyr::select(cyl, hp, wt, mpg) |>   dplyr::filter(mpg >= 20) |>   dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(hp))

title: Get started with datasetviewer
text: Export current view CSV toolbar button downloads exactly looking — visible columns, active filter, current sort, every matching row, just visible window. export streams engine row chunks, depend dataset fitting memory one piece.

what: Built for scale: no row sampling
title: Get started with datasetviewer
text: design choice makes work moving query engine browser: Transport. frame serialised Parquet nanoparquet carried widget payload — columnar, compressed, read natively engine. Engine. DuckDB-WASM reads Parquet directly answers every filter, sort, page SQL. Grid. canvas grid asks engine rows visible window (LIMIT/OFFSET query), scrolling cost independent dataset’s size. practical upshot: viewer multi-million-row frame scrolls, sorts, filters smoothly one mtcars, every row stays reachable.

title: Get started with datasetviewer
text: Shiny, pair datasetviewerOutput() UI renderDatasetViewer() server. viewer dead end: user’s current column selection, filter, sort, view mode flow back app inputs, namespaced output id, rest app can react analyst looking .
code: library(shiny) library(datasetviewer)  ui <- fluidPage(   datasetviewerOutput("viewer", height = "560px"),   verbatimTextOutput("state") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   output$viewer <- renderDatasetViewer(dataset_viewer(mtcars))    # State changes in the widget arrive as inputs, namespaced by output id.   output$state <- renderPrint({     list(       columns = input$viewer_columns, # columns currently shown       filter  = input$viewer_filter, # active filter expression       sort    = input$viewer_sort, # active sort       view    = input$viewer_view # "names" or "labels"     )   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Get started with datasetviewer
text: server required static case — vignette embeds live widgets. Drop dataset_viewer() R Markdown Quarto document result fully interactive grid. call write Shiny app produces viewer .

what: The query engine: online by default, offline when you need it
title: Get started with datasetviewer
text: Everything except one piece bundled package works internet: canvas grid, column panel, filters, code view, CSV export, data (carried page Parquet). one piece DuckDB-WASM query engine — -browser database answers every filter, sort, page. roughly 35 MB, far large ship inside R package, default widget loads public CDN (jsDelivr) first time grid rendered. interactive use connected machine, nothing needed. browser reach CDN — air-gapped laptop, corporate Shiny server behind firewall — engine must served locally. works way arrow package acquires C++ library: install time, function call. install datasetviewer, install step fetches engine (parquet extension DuckDB needs read payload) package. , Shiny app serves engine package browser — internet runtime. install machine reach public host, step skipped widget simply falls back CDN; install never fails. fetch steered environment variables, set install.packages("datasetviewer") (analogues arrow’s LIBARROW_BINARY): typical corporate deployment installs package way installs (often internal mirror already carries arrow), points variables -house mirror public host blocked, runs Shiny app — serves engine every user’s browser outbound connection. self-contained HTML document (Quarto R Markdown report, htmlwidgets::saveWidget(selfcontained = TRUE)) embeds every dependency file. Embedding 35 MB engine produce enormous page, static documents load engine CDN instead. Set option , setup chunk, dataset_viewer() call: keeps document small grid loads engine CDN reader opens . (vignette exactly .) Leave option default Shiny, engine served rather embedded.

what: SAS Studio-style dataset viewer for R
title: SAS Studio-style dataset viewer for R
text: interactive, SAS Studio-style dataset viewer R. datasetviewer renders dataset fast, scrollable grid modelled SAS Studio table viewer, one htmlwidget runs interactive Shiny apps static HTML documents. Fast large data, sampling. data sent browser Parquet queried place DuckDB-WASM, filtering, sorting, scrolling stay responsive datasets overwhelm DOM-bound grids server-paged tables. SAS Studio layout. column-selection panel char/num type icons collapse toggle, property pane (Label, Name, Length, Type, Format, Informat), names-versus-labels header toggle, header right-click sort, free-text “Filter Table Rows” expression. Clinical metadata. sibling artoo package installed, column labels, formats, informats, storage lengths read labelled CDISC-conformed frame. Plain data frames work zero artoo dependency. Runs offline / behind firewall. grid, filters, code view, CSV export bundled package. DuckDB-WASM engine loads CDN default fetched package install time, Shiny app can serve browsers internet runtime.

title: SAS Studio-style dataset viewer for R
text: Install released version CRAN: development version GitHub:

title: SAS Studio-style dataset viewer for R
text: Right-click column header sort add filter; click funnel toolbar edit filter expression directly (example AGE > 50 SEX = "M").
code: library(datasetviewer)  # a plain data frame dataset_viewer(mtcars)  # an artoo-conformed frame, headers shown as labels adsl <- artoo::read_dataset("adsl.parquet") dataset_viewer(adsl, view = "labels")

title: SAS Studio-style dataset viewer for R
code: library(shiny) ui <- fluidPage(datasetviewerOutput("viewer", height = "500px")) server <- function(input, output, session) {   output$viewer <- renderDatasetViewer(dataset_viewer(adsl))   # input$viewer_filter, input$viewer_sort, input$viewer_columns,   # input$viewer_view track the user's current view } shinyApp(ui, server)

title: SAS Studio-style dataset viewer for R
text: Everything except DuckDB-WASM query engine (~35 MB, large ship package) bundled works offline. engine loads CDN default, — like arrow package’s C++ library — fetched package install time, Shiny app can serve browsers internet runtime. Steer install-time fetch air-gapped mirrored environments: install never fails engine fetched; widget falls back CDN runtime. self-contained HTML documents, set options(datasetviewer.use_local_engine = FALSE) load engine CDN instead embedding . See vignette("datasetviewer") details.

what: View a dataset in an interactive SAS Studio-style grid — dataset_viewer
title: View a dataset in an interactive SAS Studio-style grid — dataset_viewer
text: Renders fast, scrollable data grid column-selection panel per-column property metadata. widget renders interactive Shiny apps static HTML documents.

title: View a dataset in an interactive SAS Studio-style grid — dataset_viewer
code: dataset_viewer(   x,   ...,   view = c("names", "labels"),   width = NULL,   height = NULL,   elementId = NULL )

title: View a dataset in an interactive SAS Studio-style grid — dataset_viewer
text: x Dataset view. <data.frame | character(1)>. data frame, file path read via artoo::read_dataset() (xpt, Dataset-JSON, NDJSON, Parquet, RDS). artoo-conformed frame supplies labels, formats, informats property panel; plain frame uses synthesized metadata. ... Reserved future arguments. view Initial header mode. <character(1)>. "names" (default, matching SAS Studio) shows column names headers; "labels" shows labels, falling back names label absent. width, height Widget sizing. <character(1) | numeric(1) | NULL>. Passed htmlwidgets::createWidget(). elementId Explicit DOM id. <character(1) | NULL>. Usually left NULL htmlwidgets assigns one.

title: View a dataset in an interactive SAS Studio-style grid — dataset_viewer
text: htmlwidget. Print render, use Shiny output.

title: View a dataset in an interactive SAS Studio-style grid — dataset_viewer
text: Query engine. data sent browser Parquet queried place DuckDB-WASM, filter, sort, paging run whole dataset row sampling. engine (~35 MB) loads CDN default fetched package install time reachable, Shiny app can serve browsers internet runtime. Set options(datasetviewer.use_local_engine = FALSE) force CDN (small self-contained HTML). See vignette("datasetviewer") offline corporate deployment, including DATASETVIEWER_DUCKDB_* install-time environment variables.

title: View a dataset in an interactive SAS Studio-style grid — dataset_viewer
code: # ---- Example 1: view a plain data frame ---- # # Wrap any data frame to get the interactive grid. Printing the widget in # an interactive session or a rendered document shows it; here we inspect # the payload so the example stays headless and self-contained (printing a # widget would launch a browser under R CMD check). viewer <- dataset_viewer(mtcars) viewer$x$n_rows #> [1] 32  # ---- Example 2: CDISC labels as headers ---- # # With the sibling artoo package, a CDISC-conformed frame supplies column # labels, formats, and storage lengths to the property pane and the # names-versus-labels header toggle. Start on labels with view = "labels". if (requireNamespace("artoo", quietly = TRUE)) {   labelled <- dataset_viewer(artoo::cdisc_adsl, view = "labels")   labelled$x$columns[[1]]$label } #> [1] "Study Identifier"

what: datasetviewer: SAS Studio-Style Interactive Dataset Viewer — datasetviewer-package
title: datasetviewer: SAS Studio-Style Interactive Dataset Viewer — datasetviewer-package
text: interactive dataset viewer renders fast, scrollable grid column-selection panel, per-column property metadata, names-versus-labels header toggle, modelled SAS Studio table viewer. Runs one codebase interactive Shiny apps static HTML documents, free-text row filter, header sort, CSV export, handles large datasets without row sampling querying browser 'DuckDB-WASM'.

what: Shiny bindings for the dataset viewer — datasetviewer-shiny
title: Shiny bindings for the dataset viewer — datasetviewer-shiny
text: Output render functions embed dataset_viewer() Shiny app. widget pushes live view state back Shiny inputs, app can reuse user's filter, sort, column selection server-side.

title: Shiny bindings for the dataset viewer — datasetviewer-shiny
code: datasetviewerOutput(outputId, width = "100%", height = "500px")  renderDatasetViewer(expr, env = parent.frame(), quoted = FALSE)

title: Shiny bindings for the dataset viewer — datasetviewer-shiny
text: outputId Output slot id. <character(1)>. Matched datasetviewerOutput() renderDatasetViewer(). width, height CSS sizing. <character(1)>. valid CSS size; grid fills element. expr Render expression. call returns dataset_viewer() widget. env, quoted Evaluation control. Standard htmlwidgets render plumbing; leave defaults.

title: Shiny bindings for the dataset viewer — datasetviewer-shiny
text: datasetviewerOutput() returns Shiny output UI element; renderDatasetViewer() returns Shiny render function.

title: Shiny bindings for the dataset viewer — datasetviewer-shiny
text: Inputs published. output id "viewer" widget sets, every change: input$viewer_columns (<character>): selected column names. input$viewer_filter (<character(1)>): filter expression. input$viewer_sort (<list>): sort keys (name, dir). input$viewer_view (<character(1)>): "names" "labels".

title: Shiny bindings for the dataset viewer — datasetviewer-shiny
code: # ---- Example 1: read the viewer's filter and selection server-side ---- # # The app shows a dataset and echoes the filter expression and selected # columns the user builds in the grid. Runs only in an interactive session. if (interactive()) {   library(shiny)   ui <- fluidPage(     datasetviewerOutput("viewer", height = "500px"),     verbatimTextOutput("state")   )   server <- function(input, output, session) {     output$viewer <- renderDatasetViewer(dataset_viewer(mtcars))     output$state <- renderText({       paste0(         "filter: ", input$viewer_filter, "\n",         "columns: ", paste(input$viewer_columns, collapse = ", ")       )     })   }   shinyApp(ui, server) }

title: datasetviewer 0.0.0.9000
text: Initial development version. dataset_viewer() renders SAS Studio-style interactive grid data frame, artoo-conformed frame, file path read via artoo::read_dataset(). data sent browser Parquet queried place DuckDB-WASM, filter, sort, scroll stay fast large datasets without sampling rows. shell provides column-selection panel char/num type icons collapse chevron, property pane (Label, Name, Length, Type, Format, Informat), names-versus-labels header toggle, header right-click sort size--content, free-text row filter, CSV export current view. datasetviewerOutput() renderDatasetViewer() embed widget Shiny publish current filter, sort, column selection, view mode inputs. “Show code” toolbar button emits runnable, air-formatted dplyr pipeline (select, filter, arrange, SQL--R translation) reproduces current view. DuckDB-WASM engine loads CDN default fetched package install time reachable, Shiny app can serve browsers internet runtime (offline / corporate deployment). install-time fetch honours DATASETVIEWER_DUCKDB_DIR, DATASETVIEWER_DUCKDB_URL, DATASETVIEWER_DUCKDB_EXT_URL, DATASETVIEWER_DUCKDB_OFFLINE; install never fails engine fetched (falls back CDN runtime). options(datasetviewer.use_local_engine = FALSE) forces CDN even engine present locally, keeping self-contained HTML documents small.
